groups.context_description: Term names plotting as the expression of hostile inner intention — the covert moral will directed against another, whose experience of these plots shapes the inner life of the one who endures them
Term names the counsellor as a category that the human person cannot fill for God — the recognition of the incomparable inner wisdom of God against which human wisdom is nothing
Counsel as wisdom given or received, orienting the inner person
Inner counsel of the soul in anguish — self-directed deliberation under distress
Divine counsel as God's eternal inner purpose
Rejection or spurning of divine counsel — inner failure of orientation
Counsel as wisdom given or received between persons, shaping inner orientation
Counsel as expression of inner moral character — noble, wicked, or hostile purposing
Divine counsel as God's inner intentionality directed toward persons or nations
Term names the taking of counsel as an expression of inner moral intention — the deliberation that reveals the orientation of the heart toward a moral end
Divine intimate friendship and confident disclosure
Membership in or access to the divine council/assembly
Secret plots — hidden inner scheming of the wicked
Trustworthiness in keeping intimate confidence
Term names the inner deliberative purpose or resolved intention of a person, located within the heart as the seat of purposive will
groups.notes: Anchored — dimension analytically unambiguous. C01 anchor session 2026-04-06.

groups.context_description: Term names God's sovereign purposive counsel that directs human events and vocation, intersecting with the inner life of those subject to it
groups.notes: Anchored — dimension analytically unambiguous. C01 anchor session 2026-04-06.

groups.context_description: Divine word placed in the human mouth — person as vessel of God's inner purpose
Mouth as expression of inner state — diagnostic of what is within
Mouth as site of vow and binding spoken commitment
God's own mouth as source of authoritative eternal pronouncement
Silence — inner posture of awe, humility, and restraint
Deliberate inner purposing — planning together in response to a significant situation
Counsels as product of a rebellious or wicked inner heart
Counsel as recorded wisdom addressed to the inner person

Term names the act of divine counsel as a call to attend to the inner condition — guidance that addresses the real poverty, shame, and blindness of the inner person and points toward true inner wealth
Term names the inner orientation of prudent attention — the quality of discretion and measured regard that shapes how the person attends to others and responds under pressure
Term names taste as a figure for inner discernment — the palate's capacity to test and evaluate food as the analogy for the inner faculty of testing words, truth, and the goodness of God
Term names the abstaining from taste as an expression of inner grief or penitence — the voluntary refusal to eat as the outward form of an inward state of mourning or contrition
Term names understanding as a faculty of love and spiritual orientation — the inner capacity to comprehend God's will, directed toward him in knowledge and devotion
Term names understanding as the inner reception and grasp of revealed truth — the capacity to perceive insight into mystery, to receive what God gives, and to resist what God thwarts
Term names the inner capacity of perception and discernment — the faculty by which love and knowledge translate into practical moral discrimination
Term names the inner faculty of moral discernment — the trained and exercised capacity to distinguish good from evil; discernment as a developed inner capability
Term names the inner act of reflecting or considering — deliberate inner thought directed at a situation, a vision, or a moral question
Term names the figure or imagination as an inner mental content — the inner picture the person holds of reality, whether the heart's overflow of fancies or the mind's false image of security
Term names the inner act of reflection — the thoughts and intentions of the heart as the inner content that Christ perceives and the word of God discerns
Term names the inner act of contemplative attention — the focused consideration of what is perceived in a revelatory vision
Term names the inner human capacity for discernment and moral understanding — the ability to distinguish good from evil, wisdom from folly
Term names the prayerful seeking and receiving of understanding from God — the inner posture of humility and petition before God for discernment
Term names God's inner comprehension of the human person — God's perception, understanding, and attending to the heart, thought, and deed
Term names the failure, hardening, or absence of inner understanding — spiritual blindness, incapacity to perceive God or moral reality, and the rhetorical limits of human comprehension before God
Term names the formed inner inclination of the heart — the moral orientation shaped within the person, inclining toward evil or good
groups.notes: Anchored — dimension analytically unambiguous. C01 anchor session 2026-04-06.

groups.context_description: Term names the creaturely formation known and addressed by God — the constitution of the human person as formed creature, sustained or oriented in peace
groups.notes: Anchored — dimension analytically unambiguous. C01 anchor session 2026-04-06.

groups.context_description: Term probes inner motive, spiritual orientation, or covenant posture — exposing what is in the heart
Term gives voice to inner lament, grief, or theodicy — the "why?" as cry of the suffering inner self
Knowledge as foundational inner capacity of the wise person
Knowledge of God as defining inner orientation — presence = faithfulness, absence = ruin
Knowledge as Spirit-given inner endowment — bestowed from above
Moral condition of inner intent — presence or absence determining accountability
Limits and failures of human knowledge as inner condition producing ruin
Covenantal/relational knowing between God and Israel
Self-knowledge of inner moral condition, guilt, capacity before God
God's comprehensive knowledge of the inner person — motives, character, inner states
Moral knowledge guiding right action — knowing good, evil, justice
God's revelatory knowledge — his acts, name, purposes made known
God's perfect knowledge as divine inner attribute — basis of moral judgment
Knowledge of God as content of inner formation — taught, sought, filling the person
Knowledge as inner capacity for wise governance and discernment
Thought-life or inner consciousness as moral seat of the person
Inner formed judgment or opinion held and expressed or withheld
Knowledge as inner capacity oriented toward God — measure of divine perfection
Knowledge as divine gift for wisdom and spiritual discernment
Reason as defining inner capacity — its loss the loss of humanness; restoration, dignity restored
Settled inner conviction orienting moral, spiritual, and relational life toward God
Self-knowledge of inner conflict — awareness of flesh, sin, and spiritual limitation
Identity-shaping knowledge — who the person is in Christ, transforming inner orientation
God's or another's knowledge of the inner person — hearts, thoughts, sincerity
Knowledge divorced from love — spiritually empty inner-life capacity
Term names the inner meditation or thought — the heart's offering to God and the inner scheming of adversaries
Term names meditation as the sustained inner engagement with God's law and presence — the all-day practice of the devoted heart that produces understanding
Term names the call to inner meditation and musical reflection at a moment of divine action — a pause for the heart to register what God has done
Term names the meditation of the heart as the inner activity that produces understanding — reflective inner engagement of the mind before God
Term names the inner complaint or meditation poured out before God — the sustained outpouring of anguish, petition, and reflective address that constitutes the deepest inner communication with God
Term names the inner act of remembrance — the capacity by which God, persons, or deeds are held in living consciousness; its cessation marks finality
groups.notes: Anchored — dimension analytically unambiguous. C01 anchor session 2026-04-06.

groups.context_description: Term names the memorial as moral legacy — the lasting presence or extinction of a person's name in communal memory, carrying moral weight
groups.notes: Anchored — dimension analytically unambiguous. C01 anchor session 2026-04-06.

groups.context_description: Term names the memorial — the preserved memory of love and prayer held before God and in the community, ensuring that acts of devotion are not forgotten
Term names God's sovereign inner purpose and counsel — the divine intention that precedes and governs all things, including election, calling, and salvation
Term names the human inner purpose — the settled inner resolve and purposeful aim that orients a person's life and conduct
Term names work as divinely commissioned activity — work done for or by God's direction, where inner faithfulness or slackness toward that commission is the relevant inner-being dimension
Term names work in the context of Sabbath prohibition — where the choice to abstain from work expresses an inner orientation of honour and obedience toward God
Term introduces a purpose clause disclosing an inner-being goal — where the stated purpose is that people may fear, know, love, remember, or be transformed inwardly in relation to God
Term expresses a purpose grounded in God's name, honour, steadfast love, or covenantal commitment — disclosing the inner-relational motivation of divine action on behalf of his people
Term names the answering word as an expression of inner orientation — the source and quality of a person's answer traces back to the heart, and an apt or gentle answer reflects inner disposition
Term names human inner thought and planning — the purposive deliberation of the heart, whether toward good or evil, faithful or rebellious ends
groups.notes: Anchored — dimension analytically unambiguous. C01 anchor session 2026-04-06.

groups.context_description: Term names the divine thoughts and purposes — God's inner deliberative will that exceeds human understanding and stands forever
groups.notes: Anchored — dimension analytically unambiguous. C01 anchor session 2026-04-06.

groups.context_description: Term names the goal or outcome that orients the inner person's striving and choices — the telos that gives moral meaning to action and discloses the inner directional reality of a person's life
Term names endurance to the end as the inner disposition of faithful perseverance under pressure — the completion of the inner capacity of love, hope, and fidelity held through to its telos
Term names the capacity for purposeful foresight and provision — whether expressed as governance foresight or as deliberate inner provision for desire
Term names the inner thought or intent of the heart — the purposeful inner disposition that orients a person's actions, here in the context of moral failure requiring repentance
Term names the inner purposeful scheming of the wicked — a deliberate inner plan conceived and pursued against others, contrasted with God's protective purpose
Term names purposive cause or intent — the inner purpose for which something is done, whether to disclose thoughts of the mind or to bring the living to know God's sovereignty
Term identifies deliberate inner intentionality — whether an action is purposed and willed, here probing the inner resolve behind the refusal to worship
Term names the act of reasoning and disputing as an engagement of inner faculties — where discourse aims to persuade, convince, or address the inner life of hearers, or expresses inner orientation
Term names the evil thoughts that originate in the heart — the corrupt inner reasoning that produces sin and estrangement from God
Term names inner doubts, arguments, or disputed opinions — the mind's questioning and deliberating about contested matters
groups.notes: Anchored — dimension analytically unambiguous. C01 anchor session 2026-04-06.

groups.context_description: Term names the native language as the vehicle through which inner understanding, recognition, and response are engaged — hearing in one's own language facilitates inner comprehension and attention
Term names the creative inner faculty of design — the skilled devising of form, pattern, or means, whether in craftsmanship or providential mercy
Term names inner scheming — the deliberate devising of plans directed against another
groups.notes: Anchored — dimension analytically unambiguous. C01 anchor session 2026-04-06.

groups.context_description: Intending and planning as inner acts — the inner disposition of purpose, aspiration, and design, including divine and human purposes held in the heart

Inner estimation and revaluation — the act of counting, considering, and esteeming as inner cognitive and moral acts; including the radical revaluation of loss and gain, the counting of others as more significant, and the assessment of one's own condition
Ease-induced inner contempt — the thought or inner attitude of those at ease that generates contempt for the suffering of others
Term names the inner capacity for explanation and calculation — the rational faculty seeking to understand the scheme of things
groups.notes: Anchored — dimension analytically unambiguous. C01 anchor session 2026-04-06.

groups.context_description: Term names the commanded human act of remembering — calling to mind God's acts, his covenant, one's own ways, and those in need, as the ground of present faithfulness
groups.notes: Anchored — dimension analytically unambiguous. C01 anchor session 2026-04-06.

groups.context_description: Term names divine remembering — God holding persons, his covenant, and his people in active attentiveness, with grace or judgment as the result
groups.notes: Anchored — dimension analytically unambiguous. C01 anchor session 2026-04-06.

groups.context_description: Divine knowledge of human thought — God declaring to man what his thought is, as part of the divine creative and revelatory act
Perhaps as disclosure of inner hope, fear, and intercession — the particle expressing the uncertainty of inner desire or concern when directed toward morally significant or spiritual outcomes
Term names the inner act of reasoning or questioning — the mind deliberating with itself or the heart raising doubts and questions
groups.notes: Anchored — dimension analytically unambiguous. C01 anchor session 2026-04-06.

groups.context_description: Term names deliberation and discussion among persons — reasoning together about a matter
groups.notes: Anchored — dimension analytically unambiguous. C01 anchor session 2026-04-06.

groups.context_description: Inner valuation and estimation — the act of deeming worthy or unworthy, including inner self-assessment, desire, inner determination of fitness, and the valuation of honour and calling

Judging as inner disposition and inner self-examination — the act of judging others as expressing an inner attitude, judging oneself as inner moral self-examination, and being judged as the disclosure of inner standing
Inner intention directed toward what is honourable — the inner purposing and aiming toward honourable conduct before God and others
Term names the inner faculty of thought — the mind's contents and its susceptibility to hardening, blinding, or corrupting by spiritual forces
groups.notes: Anchored — dimension analytically unambiguous. C01 anchor session 2026-04-06.

groups.context_description: Dream-visions and inner fancies — the mental images and fantasies produced in the mind during sleep, which alarm and disturb the inner being
Hidden inner thoughts perceived and known by Jesus — the inner cognitive content of persons directly accessible to divine perception
Inner thoughts as site of accusation and spiritual warfare — conflicting inner thoughts that accuse or excuse the conscience; and the capturing of every thought to the obedience of Christ

Inner reasoning and reckoning — the cognitive inner act of considering, reasoning, concluding, and the reckoning of faith, covering both practical inner determinations and the deep inner work of theological conviction
Plans that perish at death — the inner thoughts and purposes that cease when the person dies, disclosing the fragility of inner purposing
Divine giving of thought — the act of God directing cognitive attention toward persons in their need

Thoughts alarmed by revelation — the inner mental content produced by dream-visions that disturbs the person's inner state, including the thoughts the king is urged not to let alarm him and Daniel's thoughts kept in the heart
Thoughts known to and held by God — the inner thoughts of persons discerned by God from afar, and God's own thoughts precious to the psalmist

Wisdom and prudence as inner qualities — the state of being thoughtful, wise, or practically discerning; including wisdom as a character quality contrasted with folly, and being wise in one's own sight as inner pride
Shrewdness as an inner quality of practical wisdom — the adverbial expression of prudent inner discernment in worldly dealings
Royal inner planning — the king's inner decisional act of planning an appointment, disclosing an excellent spirit as the basis
Insight as a Spirit-endowed inner capacity — divine gift producing interpretive and cognitive power
Intelligence/discernment as an inner capacity — and its paradoxical limits before divine revelation
Senselessness as an inner condition — failure of understanding as a heart-state
God hears — attentive divine listening to prayer, affliction, and cry
Hearing as responsive obedience or wilful refusal — inner act of compliance or hardness toward God's word
Hearing as receptive understanding — attentive inner engagement with God's word, wisdom, or prophetic speech
Hearing producing an inner-being response — fear, grief, anger, joy, worship, courage
Understanding as a divinely given inner capacity
Understanding as an inner receptive act — the mind/heart grasping revealed truth
Lack of understanding as an inner condition — hearts hardened against perception
Understanding as a practical inner act — discerning the will of God and one's situation
Genuine knowing as an inner quality verified through the conduct of wisdom
Term names cunning or craftiness as a disposition of the inner person — the shrewd inner orientation toward deceptive self-interest
Term names the wise person as a moral character type — one whose inner orientation toward God and truth produces right conduct
Term names human wisdom as an inner disposition that can be self-deceiving — the danger of wisdom turned inward on itself
Term names prudence as a learnable inner orientation — responsiveness to correction that the simple can acquire
Term names craftiness or cunning as an inner disposition of deceptive scheming
Term names prudence as an inner capacity of discernment — enabling the person to perceive situations clearly and govern speech and conduct with care
Term names craftiness as an inner disposition of cunning — an orientation toward deceptive or self-serving ends
Term names a skilled or instructive song offered in worship — designating an act of expression whose inner devotional origin is implied by its use as a call to praise
Term names prudent wisdom as an inner capacity — understanding, considering, and perceiving wisely as an orientation toward God and truth
Term names the success and prosperity that flows from God-given wisdom — the outer result of an inner prudent orientation
Term names inner discernment or good judgment — the person's capacity to perceive situations wisely and respond with appropriate discretion
Term names prudence as an inner quality that wisdom imparts — enabling the simple to navigate life with discernment
Term names craftiness as an inner disposition of deliberate cunning — premeditated malice or strategic deception
Term names understanding as a divine attribute — the infinite discernment belonging to God by which he governs all things
Term names understanding as a human inner-being capacity — the gift from God enabling discernment of his ways and wise conduct
Term names understanding as a divine attribute — the infinite discernment by which God creates, orders, and governs all things
Term names understanding as a human inner-being capacity — enabling wisdom, right conduct, and discernment, received from God
Term names understanding as an inner-being capacity — good sense and discernment that God gives or withholds, shaping how a person relates and conducts themselves
Term names sound wisdom as a divine attribute — God's robust, reliable counsel that undergirds his governance and activity
Term names sound wisdom as an inner resource available to the upright — a substantive inner capacity that God stores for those who walk with integrity
Term designates wisdom as an inner character quality — the wise person's disposition toward knowledge, instruction, and God contrasted with the fool
Term identifies wisdom as a quality that God gives to rulers and leaders — a divine endowment of inner discernment for governance, judgment, and leadership
Term names wisdom as the inner orientation from which the fear of the Lord flows — contrasted with self-attributed wisdom as an inner posture opposed to God
Term names the act of making wise — the transformative work of Scripture on the inner person, imparting wisdom
Term names understanding as an inner-being capacity — the comprehension of God's will that enables transformed living
Term names wisdom as a divine attribute — the infinite and incomprehensible understanding belonging to God alone
Term names wisdom as the person of Christ — incarnate divine wisdom through whom God's purposes are revealed and enacted
Term names wisdom as an inner-being gift and pursuit — the human reception of divine wisdom that shapes character, speech, and conduct
Term names a divinely imposed physical condition that continues until an inner-being transformation occurs — humiliation bounded by the endpoint of inner recognition of God's sovereignty
Term names the physical mark of divine humiliation — the beast-like degradation of a person whose inner pride brought them low
Term names the terrifying physical feature of an apocalyptic power — the object of an inner-driven desire for understanding of divine mysteries
Term names the absence of wisdom as an inner-being deficit — the foolish person's lack of understanding as a moral and relational failure
Term names philosophy as a human system of wisdom — an inner orientation toward knowledge that can become a deceptive substitute for Christ
Term names the philosopher as a person oriented toward human wisdom — whose inner pursuit of knowledge encounters the gospel as foolishness
Term names wisdom as a divine attribute — belonging to God and bestowed by him as an inner capacity enabling understanding of mysteries
Term names wisdom as an inner capacity received from God — enabling reception and interpretation of divine revelation
Term names the dream as an inner-being medium — a divinely significant inner experience received during sleep that produces fear, demands interpretation, and discloses hidden things
